signals.title: USD/EUR — Hot CPI + CB Differential
signals.text: US CPI surprise reignites inflation fears, driving DXY toward 98.30 as US 10Y yields climb to 4.463%. Fed 3.75% vs ECB 2.0% — +175bp carry advantage favors USD. ECB's Nagel warns of persistent inflation, but the bear steepener regime (+56.3bp 2s10s) and hawkish Fed repricing dominate. EUR/USD at 1.1741 remains under pressure as the policy divergence widens.
signals.watch level: Watch for a break of 1.1700 following the next ECB meeting on 11 Jun.

signals.title: AUD/JPY — G8 Bilateral Theme + Carry
signals.text: AUD/JPY at 114.024. AUD 4.35% vs BoJ 0.75% — +360bp carry premium drives the bilateral G8 divergence. AUD is the week's leader (+0.553%) while JPY remains the laggard (-0.506%) despite BoJ hike bias. Retail 84% short AUD/JPY diverges from COT LF net long +58,994 (78.3% long), signaling a squeeze of retail shorts if carry demand persists.
signals.watch level: Next RBA meeting on 16 Jun determines if the carry advantage expands.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + Inflation Survey
signals.text: NZD/USD COT LF net short -19,294 (specs short) vs RBNZ hike bias priced for the next meeting. NZD long% at 18.5% represents a positioning extreme. With the RBNZ Survey of Inflation Expectations due Wednesday, any hawkish surprise risks a crowded short-unwind. NZD/USD at 0.5955 is testing the upper bound of spec conviction.
signals.watch level: RBNZ Survey of Inflation Expectations on 13 May.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Correlation Break + Carry
signals.text: USD/JPY vs US 10Y correlation at +0.594 vs 252d norm of +0.150 (z=-2.20σ). The pair is hyper-sensitized to US Treasury moves following the hot CPI print. Fed 3.75% vs BoJ 0.75% (+300bp) sustains the bid at 157.58. Carry remains the dominant driver while VIX stays at 18.0 (normal volatility), though intervention risk remains a factor as price holds near recent highs.
